Cubi Boumclap.activeSince: Behance member since 2012; La Streetarterie core member since 2015 (per regional press)
Cubi Boumclap.heroHook: French illustrator and comic artist whose indoor street-art inflected work ties comics, graphics, and urban culture—based in Agen with portfolios on Behance and cubicube.fr.
Cubi Boumclap.storyFullText:
Cubi Boumclap is listed on Behance as an illustrator, comic artist, and indoor street artist based in Agen, France, with the site cubicube.fr linked from that profile.

The Behance profile biography reads: "I'm a French illustrator, comic and digital artist. You need graphics ? Hey, I can draw stuff, so feel free to contact me, i'll be glad !" Work history entries on the same profile include roles such as illustrator, comic artist, art director, and UI designer for French studios and publishers.

Regional coverage in Le Petit Journal (Lot-et-Garonne, 2022) discusses Cubi Boumclap in the context of La Streetarterie, describing a collective promoting urban graphic culture and noting his practice blending illustration and street-influenced work.

Commercial and print channels linked from the Behance profile include Gumroad, Society6, and past shop links; The Street Lamp retail pages have listed editions attributed to the artist.

Together, public sources emphasize a hybrid practice across illustration, comics, and street-leaning graphics rather than a single gallery-only lane.
Cubi Boumclap.pullQuote: I'm a French illustrator, comic and digital artist. You need graphics ? Hey, I can draw stuff, so feel free to contact me, i'll be glad ! — Cubi _ (Behance profile, behance.net/cubi)
Cubi Boumclap.impactCallout: Documented ties to La Streetarterie and recurring illustration/comics commissions in public portfolio and press.
Cubi Boumclap.exclusiveCallout: Verify edition drops and stock solely against the artist’s own shop links and publisher pages cited on Behance/Gumroad.
Cubi Boumclap.exhibitionsText: No single consolidated exhibition CV found in the sources consulted; regional press references collective urban-art context (La Streetarterie).
Cubi Boumclap.pressText: Le Petit Journal (Lot-et-Garonne): article featuring Cubi Boumclap and La Streetarterie (2022).
Cubi Boumclap.notes: Instagram @cubi_boumclap assumed from your list; Behance does not list Instagram in the fetched profile block. Antibes sometimes appears in third-party retail blurbs—Agen is what Behance encodes as location.

Studio Giftig.location: Netherlands (duo: Niels van Swaemen, b. 1981 Eindhoven; Kaspar van Leek, b. 1983 Philipsburg, Sint Maarten)
Studio Giftig.activeSince: Collaborating since 2007; dedicated to murals full-time from 2012 (studiogiftig.nl/about)
Studio Giftig.heroHook: Dutch duo painting hyper-detailed (sur)realistic murals; they shoot their own reference so every image is autonomous. Name nods to a toxic-material paint storeroom.
Studio Giftig.storyFullText:
Studio Giftig is the Netherlands-based duo of Niels van Swaemen (born 1981, Eindhoven) and Kaspar van Leek (born 1983, Philipsburg, Sint Maarten). Their official English About page states they met in 2007 when both were already active individually in street art.

They worked from a vacant warehouse that included a storage room for toxic substances and chemicals where they kept paint and spray cans; "Studio Giftig" takes its name from that room because giftig means toxic in Dutch.

At first one member studied at art school while the other worked as a graphic designer, so projects ran evenings and weekends; in 2012 they committed full time to murals and refined a shared, highly detailed (sur)realistic style.

A defining process claim on the same site is that all images are autonomously designed: they photograph their own objects and models to maintain total control of their visual language before painting.

Third-party quotes reproduced on studiogiftig.nl include Amsterdam Street Art and Inside Information praising their mural quality and conceptual motivation; their CV page lists international mural festivals and awards such as Street Art Cities "Best mural worldwide 2022" and Dutch Street Art Awards.
Studio Giftig.pullQuote: Studio Giftig creates murals on the highest level with top quality. They speak their own visual language. Expect a well-motivated concept with their distinctive photo realistic style. — Amsterdam Street Art (quoted on studiogiftig.nl/about)
Studio Giftig.impactCallout: Street Art Cities Best mural worldwide 2022 and Dutch Street Art Awards recognition appear on the official CV.
Studio Giftig.exclusiveCallout: Autonomous image pipeline (own photography of models/objects) is the differentiator emphasized on the official About page.
Studio Giftig.exhibitionsText: Official CV at studiogiftig.nl/cv lists selected murals, festivals, and awards internationally (Europe and beyond).
Studio Giftig.pressText: Amsterdam Street Art and Inside Information quotes are embedded on studiogiftig.nl/about; Amsterdam Street Art also tags coverage of the duo.
Studio Giftig.notes: All biographical dates and the "toxic storeroom" naming story are verbatim themes from the official bilingual About page.

Unapaulogetic.location: Tel Aviv, Israel (per The Street Lamp product page and personal project site)
Unapaulogetic.activeSince: Public bios cite 10+ years in design/visual work (The Street Lamp artist blurb)
Unapaulogetic.heroHook: Multidisciplinary designer and artist behind @unapaulogetic_: motion, illustration, typography, and experimental projects like "Capturing Chaos," documented on paulrozenboim.com.
Unapaulogetic.storyFullText:
Retail and portfolio pages tie the Instagram handle @unapaulogetic_ to Paul Rozenboim, described as a Colombia-born multidisciplinary visual designer based in Tel Aviv, with work spanning motion design, illustration, typography, and installations.

His site paulrozenboim.com hosts project case studies such as "Capturing Chaos," which explains recording a double pendulum’s motion and translating it into 3D extrusions in Cinema 4D to produce sculptural stills and video art.

Another documented project, "Bring your Imagination to Life," references participation in the LaCulture Typographics Exhibition, signaling a practice that crosses gallery-oriented typography and digital craft.

The Street Lamp lists a collectible edition titled "Hole" attributed to Unapaulogetic, connecting the handle to limited-run art merchandise.

Taken together, sources emphasize generative systems, 3D motion workflows, and typographic exhibition history rather than a single static medium.
Unapaulogetic.pullQuote: The double pendulum, a simple yet chaotic system, generates unpredictable motion paths that never repeat. — Project description, Paul Rozenboim, "Capturing Chaos" (paulrozenboim.com/capturing-chaos)
Unapaulogetic.impactCallout: Work linked from a branded collectible storefront and standalone project sites with documented generative/3D process.
Unapaulogetic.exclusiveCallout: Verify any "exclusive" drops against The Street Lamp product copy and the artist’s own site—not third-party aggregators alone.
Unapaulogetic.exhibitionsText: LaCulture Typographics Exhibition referenced on paulrozenboim.com project page ("Bring your Imagination to Life").
Unapaulogetic.pressText: Primary sources: paulrozenboim.com project pages; The Street Lamp artist/product copy for "Hole."
Unapaulogetic.notes: Instagram handle confirmed on paulrozenboim.com ("Follow Me @unapaulogetic_"). Biographical "Colombia-born" appears on The Street Lamp; cross-check for future edits.

Thales Towers.activeSince: Not dated on the official one-page bio consulted
Thales Towers.heroHook: Tel Aviv street artist who paints graphic "tower-headed" figures—self-deprecating site copy says he doesn’t paint faces but loves painting towers on walls and products.
Thales Towers.storyFullText:
The artist’s own site thalestowers.com introduces Thales as being from Tel Aviv and states he does not know how to paint faces but enjoys painting towers on walls and other surfaces, mixing humor with a direct storefront for prints and objects.

Dror Hadadi’s English-language feature on drorhadadi.com summarizes Thales Towers as a Tel Aviv graffiti/street artist with a background in advertising, known for figures whose heads are buildings—work aimed at amusing passersby.

The same feature notes visible murals in areas such as Florentin and the HaSolelim complex, giving concrete city geography for locating public pieces.

Contact information published on thalestowers.com includes a Tel Aviv framing ("somewhere in Tel-Aviv"), email ThalesTowers@gmail.com, and a +972 mobile number.

GIPHY hosts a ThalesTowers channel with high view counts on animated GIFs, showing secondary distribution of the graphic characters beyond static walls.
Thales Towers.pullQuote: Thales is an attractive young old man from Tel-Aviv who doesn't know how to paint faces... he digs painting towers on walls and on other stuff — Thales (thalestowers.com homepage copy)
Thales Towers.impactCallout: Widely surfaced GIF presence on GIPHY under the ThalesTowers brand (view counts listed on GIPHY).
Thales Towers.exclusiveCallout: Shop inventory and originals should be verified on thalestowers.com product pages, which show frequent out-of-stock states in snapshots.
Thales Towers.exhibitionsText: No formal exhibition list found in the sources consulted; emphasis is on street pieces and online shop.
Thales Towers.pressText: Dror Hadadi blog feature (English) profiling Thales Towers and mural locations in Tel Aviv.
Thales Towers.notes: Hero tone follows the artist’s own humorous site voice; mural geography comes from drorhadadi.com, not municipal registries.

Nia Shtai.location: Tel Aviv, Israel (Behance, Dribbble, Ko-fi listings)
Nia Shtai.activeSince: Upcycled fashion brand narrative cites designing since 2011 (Dressed by Danielle blog interview, 2023)
Nia Shtai.heroHook: Tel Aviv illustrator and graphic designer creating Procreate-driven pop- and street-influenced work; also known for an upcycled fashion line documented in a 2023 brand feature.
Nia Shtai.storyFullText:
Professional profiles list Nia Shtai (Evgenia Batrushtay) as an illustrator and graphic designer in Tel Aviv, with Behance and Dribbble portfolios showcasing digital illustration work.

Instagram reels under @nia.shtai.art appear in search snippets promoting Ko-fi support, signaling an independent, platform-diverse practice.

A 2023 "Featured Brand" interview on dressed-by-danielle.com identifies her as a Ukrainian queer artist who fled Ukraine in 2014 and is now based in Tel Aviv, running an upcycling fashion brand alongside illustration.

That interview frames her visual world as drawing from pop culture and street art while extending into wearable pieces made from reclaimed garments.

Public-facing creative accounts (Behance, Dribbble, Ko-fi) align on city and discipline; always re-verify sensitive biographical lines with the artist before publishing.
Nia Shtai.impactCallout: Cross-disciplinary reach: digital illustration portfolios plus a documented fashion upcycling brand story in independent press.
Nia Shtai.exclusiveCallout: Clarify whether a given SKU is illustration print versus apparel; sources split across art platforms and fashion blog coverage.
Nia Shtai.exhibitionsText: No exhibition CV located in sources consulted beyond shop/brand storytelling.
Nia Shtai.pressText: Dressed by Danielle blog: featured brand interview (2023) covering background and upcycling practice.
Nia Shtai.notes: Search surfaced Instagram reel URLs (/reel/...) not /p/; omitted here per your /p/-only rule. Confirm Evgenia Batrushtay spelling with the artist.

Nurit Gross.activeSince: B.Des. Bezalel Academy of Art and Design, class of 2019 (nuritgross.com/about)
Nurit Gross.heroHook: Tel Aviv illustrator and designer blending cut paper, gouache, markers, and Procreate; award list includes Israel Museum Ben-Yitzhak children’s book prize and AOI longlisting.
Nurit Gross.storyFullText:
Nurit Gross’s about page states she is a Tel Aviv–based illustrator and designer who enjoys playful processes across mediums, pulling inspiration from art history, bold shapes, nature, quirky humor, folk art, and books.

The same page lists clients such as DOT magazine, Train Theatre, Adam Tzair, ANU museum collaborations, and municipal Tel Aviv projects, alongside magazine and newspaper names.

Exhibitions enumerated there span 2020–2023 group shows in Jerusalem and Tel Aviv (Outline, laculturetlv, illustration week, etc.).

Awards listed include the 2019 Yossi Stern Award for high achievement in illustration, the 2022 Israel Museum Ben-Yitzhak Award for children’s book illustration, 2022 3x3 Merit, and 2023 AOI World Illustration Award longlist.

Society of Illustrators Annual 65 (Hagada entry) describes her media mix: cut-out paper, pencil, gouache, marker, and digital painting in Procreate.
Nurit Gross.pullQuote: Nurit Gross is a Tel Aviv based illustrator and designer. She loves working with color, in various mediums and techniques, employing playful processes. — nuritgross.com/about
Nurit Gross.impactCallout: Major institutional recognition: Israel Museum Ben-Yitzhak Award (2022) noted on official site.
Nurit Gross.exclusiveCallout: Editorial and museum-adjacent commissions dominate the CV; verify print exclusivity per publisher contracts, not inferred.
Nurit Gross.exhibitionsText: 2020–2023 group exhibitions in Tel Aviv and Jerusalem detailed on nuritgross.com/about (Outline, laculturetlv, illustration week, etc.).
Nurit Gross.pressText: Society of Illustrators Annual 65 online feature for illustrated book work; client list includes Israel Hayom and multiple magazines on her about page.
Nurit Gross.notes: Instagram handle @nurit_gross appears in Society of Illustrators metadata; matches your list.

Moshe Gilboa.activeSince: LinkedIn cites ~10 years across UX/UI, game art, animation, illustration; Bezalel Academy studies referenced
Moshe Gilboa.heroHook: Bezalel-trained interactive artist and illustrator whose public writing on Medium documents indie games like Exploding Sheep and pandemic-era browser experiments.
Moshe Gilboa.storyFullText:
LinkedIn lists Moshe Gilboa as an Israeli creative with Bezalel Academy of Art and Design background in animation, interactive technology, video graphics, and special effects, working across UX/UI, game design, illustration, and production.

His Medium articles chronicle development posts for "Exploding Sheep," a casual mobile puzzle game, including art and design breakdowns published under @moshegilboa.

Another documented project, "How to treat your Corona," is described as a collaborative tamagotchi-style web game built during lockdown with animator Shay Adiel.

An older Blogspot (moshegilboa.blogspot.com) remains indexed as a personal creative log, complementing the Medium essays.

Public emphasis is on playful interactive work and illustrated UI/game art rather than static gallery painting.
Moshe Gilboa.impactCallout: Long-form public documentation of shipped indie game projects on Medium with process art breakdowns.
Moshe Gilboa.exclusiveCallout: Verify any fine-art editions against the artist or publisher; most indexed material is digital/game/interactive.
Moshe Gilboa.exhibitionsText: No exhibition CV found in consulted sources.
Moshe Gilboa.pressText: Self-published process writing on Medium; not independent journalism.
Moshe Gilboa.notes: Instagram @moshegilboa supplied by you; LinkedIn fetched did not surface IG URL in the snippet. Games’ availability may have changed since Medium posts.

Eden Kalif.location: Tel Aviv, Israel (multiple print shops and Artinrug)
Eden Kalif.activeSince: Post-Bezalel professional practice (Artinrug, GoodMood artist pages)
Eden Kalif.heroHook: Bezalel graduate whose spare linework carries emotion and narrative across drawings, tattoos, and prints—often photographed on rugs and print marketplaces.
Eden Kalif.storyFullText:
Artinrug’s artist page states Eden Kalif’s simple lines convey emotions, stories, and beauty, notes graduation from Bezalel Academy of Arts and Design, and describes drawing and tattooing as his main occupation while traveling with his art.

GoodMood Prints hosts an "Eden Kalif" collection with playful third-person bio lines about exploring, tattooing, and cooking spaghetti—useful tone reference but confirm before marketing use.

His own print shop domain edenkalif.com lists collections of works on paper with accessible price points for reproductions.

Third-party rug and print retailers position the same motifs in home-decor contexts, showing cross-merchandising of the line-based iconography.

Instagram linkage: Artinrug points to @____e_d_e_n____ while your brief lists @_e_d_e_n_; treat as possible handle change or variant—confirm live handle before CRM import.
Eden Kalif.pullQuote: Eden Kalif’s simple lines convey emotions, stories, and beauty. Graduating from Bezalel Academy of Arts and Design, He has made drawoinf and tattooing his main occupation, traveling the world with his art. — Artinrug artist page (note typo "drawoinf" in source)
Eden Kalif.impactCallout: Bezalel credential plus multi-channel distribution (own site, rug collaboration, print marketplaces).
Eden Kalif.exclusiveCallout: Cross-check edition claims between edenkalif.com and partners (GoodMood, Artinrug) for identical imagery and titles.
Eden Kalif.exhibitionsText: No exhibition list found in consulted commerce-first sources.
Eden Kalif.pressText: Primarily marketplace bios (Artinrug, GoodMood) rather than editorial press.
Eden Kalif.notes: Instagram handle discrepancy: Artinrug links @____e_d_e_n____ vs your @_e_d_e_n_. Artinrug bio contains a typo ("drawoinf").

Tiffany Chin.activeSince: OCAD U BDes Illustration 2018–2022; freelance illustrator from 2022 per LinkedIn/twkchin.com
Tiffany Chin.heroHook: Toronto illustrator (TWKCHIN) making bold music posters and merch; clients include Phish, Bonnaroo, Outside Lands. Stacked honors from Applied Arts, CA, and Society shows.
Tiffany Chin.storyFullText:
Tiffany Chin markets herself as TWKCHIN from Toronto, creating posters, album art, logos, merchandise, and typography inspired by 1960s–70s posters and music.

Her about page states she strives to step beyond conventional boundaries to create visually jarring imagery, and lists clients including Phish, Converse, Modest Mouse, David Byrne, Bonnaroo, Outside Lands, Mt. Joy, Goose, Ween, Scotiabank Arena, The Washington Post, and University of Toronto.

Awards enumerated there run through 2026 honors from Society of Illustrators LA, Communication Arts shortlists, Applied Arts wins, American Illustration, and 3x3 merits.

Exhibitions include Society of Illustrators annual shows in New York, Northern Contemporary in Toronto, Haight Street Art Center in San Francisco, and OCAD GradEx.

Publications range from Applied Arts and Communication Arts annuals to CBC Arts web features, documenting fast ascent after graduation.
Tiffany Chin.pullQuote: Inspired by posters and music from the 1960s and 70s, Tiffany strives to step beyond the boundaries of convention to create visually jarring imagery. — TWKCHIN (twkchin.com/about)
Tiffany Chin.impactCallout: Repeated wins in Applied Arts / Communication Arts / Society shows within three years of graduation.
Tiffany Chin.exclusiveCallout: Music-poster commissions may be editioned by bands/festivals—verify rights before calling pieces exclusive.
Tiffany Chin.exhibitionsText: Society of Illustrators NYC annuals (2022–2025), Northern Contemporary Toronto 2023, Haight Street Art Center 2024, OCAD GradEx 2022, etc. (twkchin.com/about).
Tiffany Chin.pressText: CBC Arts (2025); Applied Arts Magazine profile "Illustrator Tiffany Chin Takes the Pulse" (2025); multiple annual book inclusions listed on her site.
Tiffany Chin.notes: Awards/years on twkchin.com update frequently; re-fetch before citing "current" tallies.

Tiago Hesp.location: Portugal (Lisbon-area activity; Mistaker Maker and agenda listings reference Portuguese projects)
Tiago Hesp.activeSince: Graffiti since 1998; born 1981; scenography degree from Escola Superior de Teatro e Cinema de Lisboa (tiagohesp.com/profile)
Tiago Hesp.heroHook: Lisbon-area Portuguese artist (b.1981): murals, music, scenography, canvas. Official CV lists solos from 2011–2025 and walls like STEP IN THE ARENA, Eindhoven, 2023.
Tiago Hesp.storyFullText:
Tiago Hesp’s profile page gives a manifesto-like statement about beginning from darkness to discover light, then presents a biography: Portuguese artist born 1981 with a career spanning mural art, music, illustration, scenography, and painting.

It records scenography training at Lisbon’s theatre/film school and graffiti practice since 1998, explaining a through-line between stage space and wall painting.

The biography says he paints to resolve himself as a human being, always starting from a permanent aggressive black line and seeking balance between shadow and color.

Solo exhibitions listed run from early Lisbon boutique shows through 2025’s "É FOGO POSTO" in Porto, with many Apaixonarte and municipal gallery entries.

Collective shows and mural festivals include Underdogs Gallery programs, Mistaker Maker–curated walls, STEP IN THE ARENA 2023, and Red Bull-era Lisbon projects.
Tiago Hesp.pullQuote: To paint is, to me, a path upwards. — Tiago Hesp (tiagohesp.com/profile)
Tiago Hesp.impactCallout: Dense, dated CV of solo and collective exhibitions plus international mural festivals on the official profile page.
Tiago Hesp.exclusiveCallout: Distinction between studio canvases and commissioned murals should be tracked separately; CV mixes both.
Tiago Hesp.exhibitionsText: Solo: "É FOGO POSTO" Porto 2025; "Quase um Quarto" Mafra 2024; "Pedra e Pau" Lisbon 2023; plus earlier Lisbon/Almada/Setúbal shows. Collectives: Underdogs, Apaixonarte groupings, Abstrakt series, etc. (tiagohesp.com/profile).
Tiago Hesp.pressText: Mistaker Maker artist page summarizes practice in English; agenda cultural sites list Portuguese exhibitions.
Tiago Hesp.notes: Portuguese diacritics and venue names should be copied exactly from the official CV when localizing.

Yoaz.location: Paris, France (Hi-Fructose 2019 article; Linktree lists socials)
Yoaz.activeSince: Documented editorial coverage in Hi-Fructose (March 2019) with prior client work trajectory
Yoaz.heroHook: Paris freelance illustrator whose packed compositions mix street art, pop art, and cubist geometry—Hi-Fructose highlighted Adobe neon translations and merch-spanning applications.
Yoaz.storyFullText:
Hi-Fructose Magazine (March 14, 2019, Andy Smith) describes Yoaz’s illustrations as "abuzz with activity," like machines built from unexpected components, applied to merchandise, advertisements, Adobe neon installations, and digital displays.

The article paraphrases an artist statement that inspiration comes from street art, pop art, and cubism, and that he loves mixing colors and shapes subtly to create hallucinating detail.

Embedded Instagram posts in that article link directly to @iamyoaz permalinks, confirming social proof at the time of publication.

Linktree and portfolio hubs point to Behance and multi-platform distribution, consistent with editorial claims of broad commercial adaptation.

Yoaz should not be conflated with unrelated similarly named graffiti accounts; geography and style anchors here follow Hi-Fructose and Behance cross-links.
Yoaz.pullQuote: his inspiration comes from street art, pop art and cubism. He loves mixing colors and shapes in a very subtle way in order to create hallucinating details. — Artist statement as summarized in Hi-Fructose Magazine, March 14, 2019 (Andy Smith)
Yoaz.impactCallout: Feature coverage in Hi-Fructose plus Adobe-related translations cited in the same article.
Yoaz.exclusiveCallout: Commercial merch iterations may be licensed products; exclusivity depends on brand contracts, not the editorial piece alone.
Yoaz.exhibitionsText: Not enumerated in Hi-Fructose snippet; practice framed around commissions and installations.
Yoaz.pressText: Hi-Fructose Magazine: "The Frenetic Illustrations of Yoaz" (March 14, 2019).
Yoaz.processImage1Label: Work embedded in Hi-Fructose 2019 feature (Instagram permalink)
Yoaz.processImage2Label: Work embedded in Hi-Fructose 2019 feature (Instagram permalink)
Yoaz.notes: Pull quote is mediated through Hi-Fructose ("A statement says that …"); not a direct first-person pull from Yoaz. Process /p/ URLs are taken from embeds in that article.

TWOONE Hiroyasu Tsuri.location: Sant’Elia a Pianisi, Italy / Melbourne, Australia (hiroyasutsuri.com/about, 2021+)
TWOONE Hiroyasu Tsuri.activeSince: Born 1985 Yokohama; moved to Melbourne 2003; diploma Swinburne 2009; Berlin hub from 2014; Backwoods Gallery representation since 2010
TWOONE Hiroyasu Tsuri.heroHook: Yokohama-born TWOONE: Melbourne graffiti roots, "Raw Mark Making," museum-collected murals. CV spans Backwoods solos and giant civic walls in AU, JP, IT, CA.
TWOONE Hiroyasu Tsuri.storyFullText:
Hiroyasu Tsuri’s official biography states he was born in Yokohama, is known as TWOONE for large-scale murals, and shows in institutions including the National Gallery of Australia, Straat Museum (Amsterdam), and MUCA (Munich).

It recounts relocating to Melbourne in 2003 after high school, immersing in graffiti/street culture, coining "Raw Mark Making," and completing a Swinburne Diploma of Visual Arts & Multimedia in 2009.

Australia granted permanent residency in 2010 via a Distinguished Talent Visa the same year he joined Backwoods Gallery representation.

From 2014 Berlin served as a European hub for mural and performance collaborations; from 2021 he also bases studio practice in Sant’Elia a Pianisi, Italy.

The on-site CV enumerates solo shows from 2008’s "Thousand Can Show" through 2025 Melbourne and Nagoya projects, plus public murals in Australia, Japan, Italy, Canada, and elsewhere with linked Instagram documentation.
TWOONE Hiroyasu Tsuri.pullQuote: Immersing himself in the local graffiti and street art culture, he developed the ability to work with a wide variety of materials and techniques, coining the term ‘Raw Mark Making’ to distinguish his style. — hiroyasutsuri.com/about (official biography)
TWOONE Hiroyasu Tsuri.impactCallout: Museum acquisitions and Distinguished Talent Visa narrative appear on the official biography page.
TWOONE Hiroyasu Tsuri.exclusiveCallout: Many walls are civic commissions; editioned works flow through Backwoods Gallery—verify per release.
TWOONE Hiroyasu Tsuri.exhibitionsText: Solo: Backwoods Gallery sequences (e.g., RAW MARK MAKING 2020, THE BLUE PERIOD 2023, PHYSICAL ACTION AS THINKING FORM 2025), European gallery stops, Japanese embassy show Brussels 2019, etc. Groups: Space Invaders NGA 2010, Straat museum opening 2020, MUCA portrait survey 2018 (hiroyasutsuri.com/about CV).
TWOONE Hiroyasu Tsuri.pressText: Japan Foundation Sydney has hosted artist-talk programming under his legal name / TWOONE (event pages).
TWOONE Hiroyasu Tsuri.processImage1Label: Osaka-area mural commission (caption on hiroyasutsuri.com CV)
TWOONE Hiroyasu Tsuri.processImage2Label: Tondabayashi community-center mural (caption on hiroyasutsuri.com CV)
TWOONE Hiroyasu Tsuri.processImage3Label: Sant’Elia a Pianisi municipal mural "IN BOCA AL LUPO" (caption on hiroyasutsuri.com CV)
TWOONE Hiroyasu Tsuri.notes: Instagram /p/ URLs are copied from the official CV page’s linked documentation for specific murals. Some institution names in the long CV may carry typos (e.g., "Amsteldam")—mirror source or correct editorially.

S A R G O N.artistName: S A R G O N
S A R G O N.heroHook: No reliable source names who runs @oky.sargon. Press covers "Sargon Khnu" under other handles—do not merge identities until a primary bio links this username.
S A R G O N.storyFullText:
No authoritative biography, interview, or gallery page was retrieved that names "oky.sargon" or clearly maps @oky.sargon to a legal name, city, or exhibition history.

Search results heavily feature Sargon Khnu, a Berlin-based interdisciplinary sculptor covered by COEVAL Magazine, Metal Magazine, and Overstandard, but those pieces reference other social handles (e.g., @ssssskkkkkddddd in one interview snippet), not @oky.sargon.

Without a primary source linking the Instagram account to a named artist, any detailed life story would be speculative.

For spreadsheet hygiene, keep this row as an unverified handle until the account owner or a reputable publication explicitly connects the username to a person and practice.

Once verified, replace empty fields with dated citations from that primary source.
S A R G O N.notes: If @oky.sargon is Sargon Khnu, reconcile handles via a primary interview or Instagram bio screenshot; until then, omit Khnu facts to avoid misattribution.

kymo_one.activeSince: Active in documented murals from at least 2018 onward per Dutch Wikipedia project list
kymo_one.heroHook: Ghent muralist blending classical references with graffiti energy—Dutch Wikipedia cites large walls in Zottegem, Ghent, and a Brussels Airport tribute piece in 2020.
kymo_one.storyFullText:
Street Art Cities and Behance describe Kymo One as a Ghent-based muralist and visual designer with training in graphic illustration, working mainly in spray paint with vivid palettes.

Dutch Wikipedia enumerates notable walls such as the former Cantaert factory Zottegem (2018), Oude Beestenmarkt Ghent (2020), and a Brussels Airport Swissport-building tribute commissioned by unions ABVV/ACV during the pandemic (2020).

Behance project titles ("Surrounded," "Grinding Halt," "Rose Petals," "Abandoned Places") show recurring motifs like chains and smoke called out in encyclopedic summaries.

kymoone.be and social channels provide the living portfolio; always prefer the artist’s own captions for dates and collaborators.

Indexed Instagram search shows third-party posts crediting @kymo_one but not necessarily permalink /p/ posts on the artist feed—fetch directly from @kymo_one for compliant URLs.
kymo_one.impactCallout: High-visibility civic walls including Brussels Airport-adjacent labor tribute (2020) per Dutch Wikipedia entry.
kymo_one.exclusiveCallout: Union-commissioned murals are public; distinguish from studio originals sold privately.
kymo_one.exhibitionsText: Encyclopedia entry focuses on murals rather than white-cube shows; verify exhibitions via kymoone.be if needed.
kymo_one.pressText: Dutch Wikipedia article "Kymo One" (community-authored; corroborate dates with primary photos/captions).
kymo_one.notes: A search result showed instagram.com/gigi_dettori/p/... crediting @kymo_one; excluded from processImage fields because it is not on the artist’s account.

Tyler Shelton.location: Texas, USA (Dallas Observer profile; Austin Art Garage listings describe Austin presence)
Tyler Shelton.activeSince: Turned Instagram @sheltonartco into a business at age 20; article context at age 29 (Dallas Observer)
Tyler Shelton.heroHook: Texas pop painter mixing celebrity iconography with textured acrylic scraped by cards and screwdrivers—Dallas Observer detailed his Instagram growth and Thread Wallets collab.
Tyler Shelton.storyFullText:
The Dallas Observer reports Tyler Shelton grew up with an art-teacher mother, committed to painting in high school, and formalized promotion through @sheltonartco from age 20, later breaking out financially around 2021.

Shelton is quoted saying painting lets him share what he thinks is cool without social awkwardness: "I can show you what I think is cool or how I feel or anything without the feeling of awkwardness…"

The same feature documents process quirks—preference for inexpensive matte acrylic, scraping paint with cards, occasional gravel or ash mixed in for texture, and long iPad sketch sessions before painting.

He discusses pivoting from classic-rock portraits to a more personal illustrative aesthetic influenced by acts like Mac DeMarco, arrived at through experimentation.

Austin Art Garage and Shelton Art Company storefronts list originals and prints, extending distribution beyond Instagram.
Tyler Shelton.pullQuote: It's like a diary. When I look at one of those paintings, I almost know what I did. It makes me feel cool. It's a reference point for my life and it's something to look forward to. — Tyler Shelton (Dallas Observer interview)
Tyler Shelton.impactCallout: Six-figure Instagram following referenced in Dallas Observer; commissions from musicians/celebrities mentioned.
Tyler Shelton.exclusiveCallout: Confirm availability on sheltonartcompany.com or gallery partners; article notes rapid sell-through dynamics.
Tyler Shelton.exhibitionsText: Gallery retail via Austin Art Garage; no museum survey cited in Dallas Observer piece.
Tyler Shelton.pressText: Dallas Observer: "Dallas Painter Tyler Shelton's Instagram Is a Celebrity Obsession" (Jaci Huckeba). Tyler Paper feel-good feature on East Texas art community (2021).
